claims.claim_text: Most types do not need custom =destroy. Compiler auto-manages string, seq, ref, array, tuples, closures, auto-managed objects.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. All listed types destroyed automatically under ORC.

claims.claim_text: Compiler lifts hooks through arbitrary nesting.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Inner's =destroy called via Outer's auto-generated hooks.

claims.claim_text: Custom hooks needed for raw pointers. Without =destroy, memory leaks.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. ptr T fields not auto-managed.

claims.claim_text: Export custom hooks. Missing export is a bug.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED as best practice. Unexported work in single module.

claims.claim_text: =trace only for ORC-aware manually allocated containers participating in cycles.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. =trace compiles under ORC.

claims.claim_text: =destroy checks sentinel first. After wasMoved, destroy is no-op.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Sentinel check prevents double-free.

claims.claim_text: =wasMoved resets fields to make =destroy a no-op.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED.

claims.claim_text: Compiler synthesizes =sink from =destroy + raw move.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Synthesized sink correctly destroys old and transfers.

claims.claim_text: No self-assignment checks in =sink. Compiler eliminates x=x.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. customSinkCalled=0 for x=x.

claims.claim_text: =copy needs self-assignment protection.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Self-copy with guard preserved data.

claims.claim_text: {.error.} on =copy prevents copying at compile-time.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Move works, copy blocked.

claims.claim_text: =dup with {.nodestroy.} as optimized duplication.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Independent copy created.

claims.claim_text: lent T provides immutable borrow, no destructor injected.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED.

claims.claim_text: sink parameters are affine: consume once or not at all.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Both consuming and non-consuming sink params work.

claims.claim_text: Compiler duplicates for non-last-use sink arguments.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. copyCount=1 when source used after sink call.

claims.claim_text: Hooks must be declared before generic type usage or compiler errors.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Generic Foo[T] used before =destroy triggers 'cannot bind another =destroy' error.

claims.claim_text: importc procs do not trigger hook generation.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTABLE without C library.

claims.claim_text: Templates safely appear between type definition and hooks.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED.

claims.claim_text: move(x) forces move. Source is moved-from.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Source nil after move.

claims.claim_text: ensureMove is compile-time annotation, works for provable cases.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED for rvalues. Fails for lvalues with destructors.

claims.claim_text: =destroy takes T (not var T). Cannot assign to fields inside destroy.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Both T and var T accepted by compiler. T prevents field mutation.

claims.claim_text: =sink: destroy dest, transfer fields. No wasMoved needed in sink body after destroy.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Sink without wasMoved after destroy works for direct field transfer.

claims.claim_text: =copy: self-assign check, destroy, wasMoved, then clone.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Deep copy independent. Self-copy safe.

claims.claim_text: When =sink dest has fields not overwritten, use wasMoved before rebuild.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. wasMoved resets all fields before partial overwrite.

claims.claim_text: A move is an optimized copy when source not used afterward.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. With custom =copy, compiler may use copy instead of move for var bindings. The optimization applies when no custom =copy exists or for explicit move(). The claim is directionally correct but not guaranteed for all cases.
